title: Click event identification in delivery devices
abstract: The invention is concerned with a reliable manner of monitoring an injection process executed by means of a variable dose injection device with feedback means for generating a number of feedback events proportionate to the dose to be determined. A dialed or injected dose is determined by measuring a first signal of the process comprising a first signal peak related to a feedback event; detecting the first signal peak in the first signal; selecting an evaluation interval comprising the detected peak and adapted to an expected peak duration; deriving, from the first signal limited to the evaluation interval, a feature or characterizing parameter of the feedback event; identifying, from the derived feature, the feedback event as one of a dial up, dial down, or expel feedback event, and counting the number of identified feedback events to determine the dialed or the ejected dose. The two-stage approach with serially executed peak detection and peak identification separates an initial localization of a signal peak possibly related to a feedback event from a subsequent analysis of the detected signal peak in view of a possible source or origin feedback event, and allows to optimize data storage and processing power requirements.
claims: Claims PATENT CLAIMS 1. A method of determining a dose of a variable-dose injection device with feedback means generating a number of feedback events proportionate to the dose, comprising - measuring, by a first feedback sensor, a first signal comprising a first signal peak related to a feedback event; - optionally measuring, by a second feedback sensor, a second signal comprising a second signal peak related to the feedback event; - detecting the first signal peak in the first signal, - selecting an evaluation interval comprising the detected peak; - deriving, from the first or the second signal limited to the evaluation interval, a feature of the feedback event, - identifying, from the derived feature, the feedback event as an expel feedback event, and - counting the number of identified expel feedback events to determine an expelled dose. 2. The method of claim 1, comprising - identifying, from the derived feature, the feedback event as one of a dial up or dial down feedback event, and - counting the number of identified dial up and dial down feedback events to determine a dialed dose. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, comprising - deriving the feature from the second signal. 4. The method of one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the first and the second feedback sensor are mechanical sensors selected from the group comprising an accelerometer, a gyroscope, a piezo contact microphone. 5. The method of one of claims 1 to 4, comprising - defining, in a measured signal, successive data frames of pre-defined length, - prepending signal data of a selected evaluation interval in a first data frame to a second data frame if the selected evaluation interval extends into the second data frame, and - processing the signal data of the selected evaluation interval within the second data frame. 6. The method of one of claims 1 to 5, comprising - deriving, from the first or the second signal limited to the evaluation interval, a non-event feature, and - identifying, from the derived non-event feature, the first or second signal as a false positive. 7. The method of one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the first or the second feedback sensor is a multi-axis gyroscope, and wherein deriving the feature comprises - calculating, for each axis of the gyroscope, a correlation value of a test signal with a single-axis gyroscope signal, and adding the correlation values; or - calculating, for each axis of the gyroscope, a sum of absolute derivatives of a single-axis gyroscope signal, and adding the sums. 8. An electronic module for a variable dose delivery device comprising - a first feedback sensor (21) adapted to measure a first signal comprising a first signal peak related to a feedback event generated by a feedback means of the variable dose delivery device (1), - optionally, a second feedback sensor adapted to measure a second signal comprising a second signal peak related to the feedback event, and - a processing unit (22) configured to - detect the first signal peak in the first signal, - select an evaluation interval comprising the detected peak; - derive, from one of the first and the optional second signal limited to the evaluation interval, a feature of the feedback event, and - identify, from the derived feature, the feedback event as one of a dial up, dial down, or expel feedback event. 9. The electronic module of claim 8, comprising attachment means (14) for releasable attachment of the electronic module to a main housing part (10) of the variable dose delivery device. 10. The electronic module of claim 8 or 9, comprising a board with a printed circuit (26) and a battery (27) with a main surface arranged parallel to the board, wherein the battery is accommodated in a recess of the board and extends to both sides of the board. 11. The electronic module of one of claims 8 to 10, comprising a module housing with an opening allowing a user to read a dose dialed and depicted in a window of the delivery device, wherein a border of the opening (25) is illuminated. 12. The electronic module of one of claims 8 to 11, comprising an accelerometer or a gyroscope as a feedback sensor, wherein the signal processing unit is configured to evaluate a signal measured independent of a dose setting or dose expel process of the delivery device to detect a device handling activity of the user. 13. The electronic module of claim 12, wherein the signal processing unit is configured to track, from the evaluated signal and during an expel process, a position of the delivery device, and to acknowledge the expel process as an injection process if the tracked position is between two co-centric spherical surfaces distant lees than a penetration depth of the delivery device. 14. The electronic module of one of claims 8 to 13, comprising an accelerometer as the first feedback sensor and a gyroscope as the second feedback sensor, wherein the processing unit is configured to derive the feature of the feedback event from the second signal. 15. The electronic module of one of claims 8 to 14, wherein - the first feedback sensor (21) is adapted to measure the first signal at a sampling rate of no more than 10 kHz, - the processing unit (22) is configured to process the measured first signal in data frames of a length of at least 2 ms, and to select an evaluation interval of a length D of no more than the length of the data frames.
